data.title: National Fuel Gas Q2 adjusted EPS rises but trims 2026 profit outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • US natural gas utility and producer's fiscal Q2 adjusted EPS rose 13% from prior year

  • Company revised fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS guidance downward, citing lower gas price assumptions

  • Higher realized gas prices boosted results, but production volumes fell due to weather delays

Outlook

  • Company lowers FY 2026 adjusted EPS guidance to $7.45-$7.75

  • Company now assumes NYMEX natural gas price will average $3.00 per MMBtu for remainder of FY 2026

  • Integrated Upstream and Gathering FY 2026 production expected at 425-440 Bcf, down from prior guidance

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER REALIZED GAS PRICES - Co said a 17% increase in realized natural gas prices drove adjusted earnings growth in Integrated Upstream and Gathering segment

  • LOWER PRODUCTION VOLUMES - Co said weather-driven completion delays and typical declines on producing wells led to a 3% decrease in natural gas production

  • UTILITY RATE INCREASES - Higher customer margin in Utility segment attributed to new rates in New York and system improvement charges in Pennsylvania

Company press release:

data.title: Republic Airways Q1 revenue rises, helped by Mesa merger
data.description:

data.title: S&T Bancorp Raises Quarterly Cash Dividend by 2.8%
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

S&T Bancorp said its board approved a 2.8% increase to the company's quarterly dividend.

The dividend of 37 cents per share will be payable on May 28 to shareholders of record on May 14. The company last raised its dividend to 36 cents per share in October.

The new dividend, $1.48 on an annual basis, represents a yield of 3.4%, based on Wednesday's closing share price of $43.77.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: PulteGroup Announces Quarterly Cash Dividend of $0.26 Per Share
data.description:
ATLANTA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 29, 2026--

PulteGroup, Inc. announced today that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.26 per common share payable July 2, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on June 16, 2026.

About PulteGroup

PulteGroup, Inc. , based in Atlanta, Georgia, is one of America's largest homebuilding companies with operations in more than 45 markets throughout the country. Through its brand portfolio that includes Pulte Homes, Centex, Del Webb, DiVosta Homes, and John Wieland Homes and Neighborhoods, the company is one of the industry's most versatile homebuilders able to meet the needs of multiple buyer groups and respond to changing consumer demand. PulteGroup's purpose is building incredible places where people can live their dreams.

For more information about PulteGroup, Inc. and PulteGroup brands, go to pultegroup.com; pulte.com; centex.com; delwebb.com; divosta.com; and jwhomes.com. Follow PulteGroup, Inc. on X: @PulteGroupNews.

data.title: TSX extends daily losing streak as metal mining shares fall
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends down 0.8% at 33,318.39
  • Bank of Canada holds benchmark rate at 2.25%
  • Materials group loses 2.3% as gold falls
  • Oil settles nearly 7% higher boosting energy

By Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index fell on Wednesday to a three-week low, as rising uncertainty about the duration of the Middle East conflict unnerved investors, with railroad and metal mining shares among the biggest decliners.

The Toronto Stock Exchange's S&P/TSX composite index ended down 265.95 points, or 0.8%, at 33,318.39, marking its lowest closing level since April 7. It was the fifth straight daily decline for the index, the longest losing streak since December 2024.

"Middle East tensions are resurfacing ... so we have more of a risk-off environment," said Michael Dehal, a senior portfolio manager at Dehal Investment Partners at Raymond James.

U.S. President Donald Trump discussed how to mitigate the impact of a possible months-long U.S. blockade of Iran's ports with oil companies, a White House official said.

"As this war drags on, the inflation expectations continue to rise up and that's going to cause markets to price in interest rate hikes from central banks," Dehal said.

The Bank of Canada left its benchmark interest rate on hold at 2.25% but said if oil prices stayed high and began pushing up inflation, it might have to respond with consecutive rate hikes.

At least two rate increases this year have been priced into the market by investors. (0#CADIRPR)

The materials sector , which includes Canadian metal miners, was down 2.3%. The price of gold fell 1.1% to hit a near one-month low as the U.S. Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged as expected.

Industrials lost 1.6%, with shares of Canadian National Railway Co down 6% after the company's earnings missed estimates.

CGI Inc met earnings expectations. Still, shares of the information technology consulting services company ended 10.8% lower.

Energy added 2.7% as the price of oil settled nearly 7% higher at $106.88 a barrel.

data.title: Allstate quarterly profit jumps on sharp drop in catastrophe losses
data.description:
Allstate reported a jump in first-quarter profit on Wednesday as the property and casualty insurer benefited from a sharp fall in catastrophe losses.

Earnings rose across the insurance industry on weaker catastrophe losses in the quarter. The prior year's quarter results were affected by the impact of the California wildfires.

Here are some details:

  • Property-liability unit catastrophe losses shrank to $1.24 billion in the quarter, compared with $2.20 billion a year earlier.

  • Net income applicable to common shareholders was $2.43 billion in the three months ended March 31, compared with $566 million a year earlier.

  • Net investment income jumped to $938 million in the quarter from $854 million a year earlier, underpinned by strong gains in its market-driven investment portfolio.

  • "Market share of auto and homeowners insurance increased in many states due to a comprehensive approach of more affordable prices, new products, expanded benefits, bundled offerings, lower expenses, sophisticated analytics and increased marketing," CEO Tom Wilson says.

  • Property-liability unit combined ratio was 82% in the quarter, compared with 97.4% a year earlier. A ratio below 100% indicates an insurer earned more in premiums than what it paid out in claims.

data.title: Microsoft Stock Slips Despite Earnings Beat — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Microsoft stock was trading slightly lower Wednesday night after the tech giant beat financial estimates for its fiscal third quarter.

Microsoft posted adjusted earnings of $4.27 a share on revenue of $82.9 billion, which is higher than earnings estimates of $4.05 a share on revenue of $81.4 billion, according to FactSet.

Shares were down 2.7% in after hours trading.

This is breaking news. Please come back for more updates and analysis and read below for a preview of the earnings.

Microsoft shareholders will be watching for cloud revenue growth, capital spending expectations, and Copilot monetization when the tech giant reports earnings Wednesday.

Microsoft is scheduled to report fiscal third-quarter financial results after the stock market closes. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect the company to post adjusted earnings of $4.05 a share on revenue of $81.4 billion. That would be higher than the earnings of $3.46 a share on revenue of $70.1 billion Microsoft reported in the same period last year.

Microsoft is among a group of the world's largest tech companies that are spending billions of dollars to build out the infrastructure needed to power artificial intelligence. Wall Street initially celebrated plans to invest big in the evolving tech, but that sentiment has shifted as investors want to see more of a return on this spending that has impacted free cash flows.

Analysts expect Microsoft to report third-quarter capex of $37.5 billion and free cash flow of $15.4 billion. That's compared with last year's third-quarter capex of $21.4 billion and free cash flow of $20.3 billion.

One way Microsoft can show that there's strong demand for AI that justifies its massive spending initiatives is through Azure cloud revenue growth. Wall Street is expecting Azure revenue growth of 39.7%, which would be up from the prior quarter's 39% growth. This is a metric investors watch closely, so anything less than a beat could be problematic for the stock.

Cloud growth may feel the pressure of capacity constraints as demand outpaces supply. Companies are working to get servers and data centers up and running to meet that demand, but "there is a limit to the amount of capacity they and their partners can deliver near-term," Deutsche Bank analyst Brad Zelnick wrote in a note on April 20. He rates Microsoft as a Buy with a $575 price target and believes this could lead to capex rates slowing through fiscal 2027.

It's about more than just cloud growth when it comes to boosting investor sentiment. Microsoft stock is down 12% this year. One reason for this is concerns over how evolving AI models can replace critical software functions. Microsoft, which has a massive enterprise software business, needs to prove to shareholders that it's attracting strong demand for its Copilot AI product to help assuage some of those fears.

The company said last quarter that paid M365 Commercial seats grew to over 450 million, with 15 million paid Microsoft 365 Copilot seats. Copilot seat growth is an important metric for investors to see additional AI monetization.

Investors will also be listening closely to management commentary after Microsoft and the OpenAI announced on Monday that the two companies no longer have an exclusive partnership.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Nigeria's Tinubu nominates new oil regulator in second leadership change in four months
data.description:
By Camillus Eboh

Nigerian President Bola Tinubu has nominated Rabiu Abdullahi Umar as chief executive of the Nigerian Midstream and Downstream Petroleum Regulatory Authority (NMDPRA), the second leadership change at the petroleum regulator in four months, the presidency said on Tuesday.

Umar replaces Saidu Mohammed who was appointment in December after their predecessors abruptly quit, amid a high stakes clash between one agency and Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote.

Wednesday's nomination comes as Nigeria grapples with rising domestic energy prices, partly driven by higher global oil prices following the escalation of conflict involving Iran, which has heightened concerns about supply disruptions and increased volatility in international energy markets.

The presidency said the decision was made in the public interest and aimed at strengthening regulatory effectiveness in the midstream and downstream petroleum sector.

Pending Senate confirmation, the most senior official at the NMDPRA will oversee the agency in an acting capacity.

Umar has more than 25 years of experience across the energy, manufacturing and infrastructure sectors. He previously worked at Dangote Cement, Nigeria’s largest cement producer, and has held senior roles involving operational management and large-scale project delivery.

The NMDPRA was established under a new law in 2021 to regulate Nigeria’s midstream and downstream petroleum operations, a critical segment of Africa’s largest oil-producing economy.

data.title: Soybean, corn barge bids steady to lower
data.description:
Spot basis bids for corn and soybeans shipped by barge to U.S. Gulf Coast terminals were steady to lower on Wednesday, traders said.

  • Chicago Board of Trade corn futures , buoyed by strong export demand and slight weather concerns in the U.S. corn belt, settled 2-1/4 cents higher at $4.77-3/4 per bushel. Earlier in the session, the most-active contract on a continuous chart hit a near one-year high of $4.79 per bushel.

  • CBOT soybean futures settled 7-3/4 cents higher at $11.97 per bushel.

  • Early U.S. soybean and corn planting has been progressing well, although storms expected in the U.S. Midwest could delay seeding in some areas, traders and market analysts said.

  • U.S. agribusiness Bunge Global raised its full-year adjusted profit forecast on Wednesday, citing strong oilseed processing margins and an improved biofuel demand outlook after the U.S. raised its biofuel blending mandate.

  • Gulf FOB markets continued to be steady on export demand and U.S. farmers turning their focus to spring planting, dealers said.

  • CIF Gulf corn barges loaded in April were bid at 96 cents over CBOT May (CK26) corn futures, down 1 cent from Tuesday. May barge loadings were bid at 95 cents over futures, steady from Tuesday.

  • Gulf corn barges loaded in May traded at 96 cents over May corn futures.

  • FOB corn vessels loaded at the Gulf in June were offered at around 100 cents over CBOT July (CN26) futures, steady from Tuesday.

  • For soybeans, CIF Gulf barges loaded in April had no bids for a second day, while May loadings were bid at 94 cents over CBOT May (SK26) soybean futures - down 4 cents.

  • FOB export premiums for June soybean vessels at the Gulf held steady at around 96 cents over July (SN26) futures.

For displays of CIF basis, click on the codes in brackets:
